flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
πίστις — "faith" (subjective trust) vs. "faithfulness" (objective reliability). "Faith of Christ" (G1342 Χριστοῦ) may mean trust IN Christ or the faithfulness OF Christ.
σάρξ — "flesh" (physical body, neutral) vs. "sinful nature" (Paul's ethical sense). Using one English word across both contexts either over- or under-interprets Paul.

flags.note: δικαιοσύνη — "righteousness" (moral attribute) vs. "justification" (legal declaration). The Reformation turned on this word: is it a quality God gives or a status he declares?
Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
νόμος — "law" (general moral law) vs. "the Law" (Torah specifically) vs. "a law" (a principle). Context determines which, but translators must choose.
χάρις — "grace" (unmerited favor) vs. "favor" (patron's gift in social context). The patron-client background charges this word with economic and social concreteness.

flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
νόμος — "law" (general moral law) vs. "the Law" (Torah specifically) vs. "a law" (a principle). Context determines which, but translators must choose.
πίστις — "faith" (subjective trust) vs. "faithfulness" (objective reliability). "Faith of Christ" (G1342 Χριστοῦ) may mean trust IN Christ or the faithfulness OF Christ.
σάρξ — "flesh" (physical body, neutral) vs. "sinful nature" (Paul's ethical sense). Using one English word across both contexts either over- or under-interprets Paul.
